title: Source to Shore route-flow consolidation
summary: Align Source to Shore active, ready, filed, station, map, journal, atlas, support, and debug-snapshot behavior around the new container without adding breadth.

execution_order.goal: Map the Source to Shore active, ready, filed, support, map, journal, atlas, and snapshot touchpoints now that the station container exists.
execution_order.why_now: The beta chapter should feel like one route arc instead of scattered late-game exceptions.

execution_order.goal: Consolidate the route-flow behavior and add focused regression coverage for all three existing beats.
execution_order.why_now: Stable state flow is required before route data or game coordination extraction is safe.

execution_order.goal: Review the flow for state consistency, debug snapshot truth, route-marker behavior, and no new chapter breadth.
execution_order.why_now: This packet decides whether the three-beat beta is ready for architectural cleanup.

completion_gate: Source Shelter, Forest Release, and Coastal Catch route states agree across station, map, journal, atlas, support, and debug snapshots.
Filed Source to Shore remains a calm chapter-end state, not an invitation into a hidden fourth beat.
Regression coverage proves representative active, ready, and filed Source to Shore states.
No new route framework, save schema, planner, or fourth beat is introduced.
guardrails: No fourth Source to Shore beat.
Consolidate existing behavior before extracting data.
Do not broaden Route v2 semantics unless scout identifies a failing existing contract.
Keep route-marker and support-slot behavior tied to the existing support seams.
Keep copy compact and exact-copy-test any changed player-facing line.

scout_contract.implementation_dependency: Keep ECO-20260428-main-451 parked until ECO-20260428-critic-450 approves the dedicated Source to Shore station container.
scout_contract.state_source_of_truth: Keep src/engine/source-to-shore-state.ts as the single Source to Shore display-state resolver. A compact local state matrix or helper is acceptable if exported behavior stays stable.
touchpoints: src/engine/field-requests.ts owns the three Route v2 route definitions, evidence ids, slot order, note text, and live process variants.
src/engine/source-to-shore-state.ts maps completed/request-progress state into active, ready-to-file, and final filed station/map/journal/atlas copy.
src/engine/field-season-board.ts consumes Source to Shore state for the route board, launch card, atlas note, archive strip, and expedition card.
src/engine/field-season-outing-locator.ts and src/engine/field-request-state.ts carry active outing, journal, route-marker, and world-map replay behavior.
src/engine/field-request-controller.ts carries support-biased inspect and hint behavior through existing hand-lens, note-tabs, place-tab, and route-marker seams.
src/engine/debug-save-snapshots.ts and src/test/save-snapshots.test.ts carry the active, ready, downstream, and final filed debug contract.
main_451_scope: Make the three-beat Source to Shore state matrix explicit enough that station, atlas, map, journal, support, and debug snapshots consume the same active/ready/filed truth.
Preserve Source Shelter, Forest Release, and Dune Catch route ids, evidence ids, ordered slots, process variants, support behavior, and save schema.
Keep Dune Catch as the route title/id and reserve Coastal Catch for final filed memory language.
Keep ready-to-file states map-calm with no route-marker target and no active world-map focus.
Keep final filed Source to Shore as a calm chapter-end state with no active outing or implied fourth beat.
test_contract: npm test -- --run src/test/field-requests.test.ts src/test/field-request-controller.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|Cool Release|Rime Source|route-marker|hand-lens|note-tabs"
npm test -- --run src/test/field-season-board.test.ts -t "Source to Shore|copy budget|station atlas and active outing copy"
out_of_scope: No fourth Source to Shore beat.
No route catalog extraction; packet 171 owns that after packet 170 is reviewed.
No save-schema, planner, dashboard, inventory, or support-system expansion.
No route id, evidence id, slot-order, or filed-state rename unless a failing focused test proves the current contract is wrong.

post_packet_path: Route catalog extraction can proceed after this flow review is clean.
Content and spatial payoff can use this packet's state map as their baseline.
